visual_checks: Attempt to use all interactive elements (links, buttons, menus, form controls, sliders, drag-and-drop) using only the keyboard
Check that all functionality available via mouse or touch is also available via keyboard with no exceptions
Verify that custom widgets (date pickers, carousels, accordions, modals) are fully operable by keyboard
Look for drag-and-drop interfaces and verify keyboard alternatives exist for all drag operations
Check that drawing or path-dependent interactions have keyboard-accessible equivalents
Verify that no functionality requires specific pointing device gestures (e.g., multi-touch, pressure-sensitive) without a keyboard alternative
pass_conditions: All functionality on the page is operable through a keyboard interface with no exceptions
Custom interactive widgets (sliders, drag-and-drop, carousels, date pickers) are fully keyboard accessible
No feature requires a pointing device, touch gesture, or path-dependent input without a keyboard alternative
All interactive elements can be reached, activated, and operated using standard keyboard controls (Tab, Enter, Space, Arrow keys, Escape)
Drawing or freeform input features provide keyboard-accessible alternatives
fail_conditions: Any functionality is only operable by mouse, touch, or gesture with no keyboard alternative
Custom widgets (drag-and-drop, sliders, color pickers) cannot be operated via keyboard
Interactive content requires specific pointing device features (hover-based actions, multi-touch gestures) with no keyboard equivalent
Some interactive elements are unreachable or inoperable via keyboard navigation
Path-dependent input (drawing, freeform annotation) has no keyboard-accessible alternative
na_conditions: Page contains no interactive elements beyond basic browser-handled links and form controls that are inherently keyboard accessible
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Page content failed to load so keyboard operability cannot be evaluated
common_mistakes: Drag-and-drop reordering interfaces with no keyboard-based alternative for moving items
Custom sliders or range controls that only respond to mouse dragging
Map interactions (zoom, pan, pin placement) that lack keyboard controls
Canvas-based drawing tools with no keyboard or text-based input alternative
Hover-triggered menus or tooltips that cannot be activated via keyboard focus
Touch-only gestures (swipe, pinch-to-zoom) on web content with no keyboard fallback
Custom video players with controls that only respond to mouse clicks

examples.pass: A project management board with drag-and-drop cards also provides keyboard shortcuts and a move-to menu to reorder and reassign cards, ensuring all functionality is keyboard accessible
examples.fail: A photo editing tool allows cropping and drawing only through mouse interactions with no keyboard-accessible alternatives for any editing feature
examples.partial: Most page functionality is keyboard accessible, but a drag-and-drop file uploader and an interactive map widget have no keyboard alternatives

plain_meaning: This is the AAA version of SC 2.1.1 with no exceptions. ALL functionality must be keyboard-operable, including functions that 2.1.1 exempts because they depend on path of motion. The intent is full keyboard parity with pointer input for every user.
scope_applies_to: Freehand drawing, signature capture, and handwriting input
Gesture-based controls (swipe, pinch, rotate) of any kind
Any path-dependent interaction that 2.1.1 would exempt
borderline_calibration: Apply the same rules as 2.1.1 but remove the path-dependent exemption.
A drawing canvas with no keyboard alternative fails 2.1.3 even if it passes 2.1.1.
If 2.1.1 passes and there are no path-dependent features, 2.1.3 also passes.
Do not demand pixel-identical behavior -- a keyboard alternative that achieves the same end result is sufficient.
